prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
Item "1C. Cybersecurity" ENDS on this page; the next section is "Item 2. Properties".
First locate where Item 1C stops on this page:
- If the "Item 2" heading is visible on this page, Item 1C ends on the line directly ABOVE that heading.
- Ignore ALL text below that heading; it belongs to the next section, not Item 1C.
- The target section may have started on a previous page and may leave only a short carryover at the very TOP of this page.
- If the next-section heading appears near the top, the answer may be only the few lines immediately above that heading.
- Only if no later section heading appears on this page should you use the bottom of the page as Item 1C's ending.
Then transcribe VERBATIM the LAST 5 lines of Item 1C right before the section ends. The answer must come only from Item 1C, even if the next section occupies most of the page. Ignore running headers, page numbers, footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
Director, Security, Privacy & Assurance on various cybersecurity matters, including risk assessments, mitigation strategies, areas of emerging risks,
incidents and industry trends, and other areas of importance.
